topTargets.name: Chris Brazzell II
topTargets.reasoning: Perfect 1.15 fit for an RSP-high GM: Consensus #15 Tier 2 (likely available), but RSP DoT 84.2 ranks him #12 with a George Pickens comp — Waldman calls out 'High ceiling with Pickens-like traits.' Carolina landing spot is a target-share opportunity. WR room is deep (7) but mostly in win-now; this is the upside swing my weighting demands.

topTargets.name: Chris Bell
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #19 Tier 2, RSP DoT 83.9 (#16) with an A.J. Brown high-end comp — RSP says 'Wait for second round if falls' but at 1.15 he's right in the slot. Miami landing is fine. With my 40% RSP weight, the Brown comp plays louder than ADP 28.24 suggests.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #17 Tier 2, RSP DoT 84.1 (#14), TEN landing. RB depth is 6 but contracts shrink fast — Singleton hits both win-now (Tier 2 talent) and dynasty (RSP Rotational Starter grade). Safer floor than Brazzell/Bell if I want a back at 1.15.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Prime 3.02 RSP-special: Consensus #24 Tier 4 but RSP DoT 83.9 (#18) with a Jayden Reed comp — Waldman literally tags him 'a potential bargain.' WAS landing. ADP 29.06 confirms market hasn't caught up. With my 50/40/10, Williams jumps two tiers above Consensus.

topTargets.name: Elijah Sarratt
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #20 Tier 3, RSP DoT 83.9 (#17) — RSP names him 'safest option by RSP scoring among WRs 10-16.' BAL landing has long-term target room. At 3.04 this is a value floor pick that aligns with Waldman's safety bias.

capPosture: Cap-tight at $40.7M used / $4.3M space — all three rookies must go taxi (50% hit = ~$500K each fits; full active rookie deals would not).

wildcard.name: Bryce Lance
wildcard.reasoning: Pure RSP-high GM swing at 3.04: Consensus has him at #38 (Free Agents tier), but RSP DoT 83.5 (#22) with an A.J. Green high-end comp and 'must-draft' tag in deep leagues. NO landing. 40% RSP weight makes him a defensible reach where most GMs won't blink until round 4.

summary: Wascawy Wabbits is a deep, cap-strapped roster (23 players, $4.3M space, only 2 expirings) with no urgency to active-roster rookies — every 2026 pick goes to taxi. With high RSP affinity, the play at 1.15 is a Waldman-favored upside WR (Brazzell II or Bell) over a positional reach, then mining 3.02/3.04 for RSP sleepers like Antonio Williams (Reed comp) and Sarratt/Lance where my 40% RSP weighting reaches farther than the Consensus crowd. This is a developmental class — stockpile A/B-tier RSP graded talent on the cheap taxi shelf and re-evaluate next summer.
